title: Legal and Other Requirements Procedure
preamble: This procedure governs how the owner-led project identifies, accesses, communicates, and monitors compliance with all applicable legal and HSESC obligations across all project locations and lifecycle phases. In the owner-led model RT holds direct legal responsibility and cannot delegate compliance accountability — this procedure establishes how that accountability is exercised across all project participants.
references: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0010 – Document Control (register format and retention)
referenced_by: All procedures — each references the Legal Register as the source of applicable legislation

requirements.text: Define the method for continuously identifying, tracking, and updating regulatory requirements across all locations where Work is performed, including a Regulatory Requirements Register as the primary compliance management tool.

Specify that the register must be submitted to the relevant project authority within 30 days of the Effective Date, and that roles and responsibilities for compliance are clearly defined within the register for all applicable obligations.

Include within the register all permits, approvals, conditions of consent, and commitments made through Environmental and Social Impact Assessment processes — these carry equal standing to legislation and must be tracked as binding obligations throughout the project lifecycle.

Where host country laws and regulations are less stringent than IFC Performance Standards or World Bank EHS Guidelines, document the more stringent requirement in the register as the project standard.

Confirm that the project commits to full compliance with applicable national and international laws, regulations, and other requirements to which the organisation subscribes including voluntary commitments.

requirements.tag: [TSM – TSM – Safe, Healthy and Respectful Workplaces Protocol, Indicator SH1]
requirements.text: Ensure the register captures all applicable occupational health and safety legislation relevant to the project jurisdiction and that senior management accountability for its currency is clearly assigned.

Define the process for continuously monitoring regulatory changes: subscriptions to government gazette services; engagement with legal counsel; and participation in relevant industry body notifications. Require formal review at minimum quarterly and following any legislative change, permit amendment, or approval condition update.
Assign a named accountable role with authority to ensure the register is current and that identified compliance obligations are actioned within defined timeframes.

The project must define escalation thresholds for non-compliance events — distinguishing between minor administrative non-conformances, reportable breaches, and enforcement actions — and specify the response timeframe and notification chain for each level.
Define how newly identified or changed legal requirements are communicated to affected project personnel, supervisors, and all contractor organisations working under the project management system.
Ensure that relevant legal requirements are communicated to contractors through contractual clauses and that contractor compliance is monitored through the project ESMS.
Ensure workers have access to information about the legal and regulatory requirements applicable to their work, including their rights under applicable health and safety law.
Require prompt reporting of all regulatory non-compliances, government notices, and enforcement actions to the project leadership team and to the relevant corporate function.

Ensure that any identified non-compliance with applicable laws, regulations, or permit conditions triggers a corrective action process and is documented in the project action tracking system with a defined closeout date and owner.

project_completion_guidance: Identify and list all jurisdictions in which this project operates and insert into the register template (RTPR-HSE-REG-0001). Consider federal, state/territory, and local government levels.

Define the specific monitoring mechanism to be used for this project (e.g., named subscription service, legal counsel engagement arrangement, peak body memberships) — the procedure states a process is required but the specific service is a project decision.

Define the non-compliance escalation matrix for this project: what constitutes a minor non-conformance vs a reportable breach vs an enforcement action, and who is notified at each level. Insert into the designated table in this procedure.
Identify the role responsible for maintaining the register by name and position title for this project.
Insert the project-specific submission timeline for the initial register to the project owner — check the project Deliverables Schedule (RTPR-HSE-REG-0002).

external_sources.summary: Requires a continuous method for identifying, tracking, and updating regulatory requirements, with formal review at minimum quarterly and following any legislative or permit change.

Requires submission of the regulatory requirements register to the project authority within 30 days of the Effective Date, with clearly defined roles and responsibilities for compliance, and communication of changed requirements to all project participants.
Requires that all permits, approvals, conditions of consent, and ESIA commitments are included in the register and tracked as binding obligations throughout the project lifecycle.
Requires that where host country laws are less stringent than IFC Performance Standards or World Bank EHS Guidelines, the more stringent requirement is documented as the project standard.
Requires that legal requirements are communicated to contractors through contractual clauses and that contractor compliance is monitored through the project ESMS.

Requires that any identified non-compliance with applicable laws, regulations, or permit conditions triggers a corrective action process documented in the project action tracking system with a defined closeout date and owner.
Requires that the project commits to full compliance with applicable national and international laws, regulations, and voluntary commitments to which the organisation subscribes.

external_sources.clause: TSM – Safe, Healthy and Respectful Workplaces Protocol, Indicator SH1
external_sources.summary: Requires that all applicable occupational health and safety legislation is captured in the register and that senior management accountability for its currency is clearly assigned.
Requires assignment of a named accountable role with authority to ensure the register is current and compliance obligations are actioned within defined timeframes.
Requires that workers have access to information about the legal and regulatory requirements applicable to their work, including their rights under applicable health and safety law.
